授業目的:
This is designed to help students understand how and why consumers make choices by examining the psychological, social, cultural, and digital factors that influence everyday decisions. Through real‑world cases and interview data, students analyze
consumers as both individuals and decision makers and critically synthesize
insights from these cases.
到達目標: 1.	Students will develop and apply practical understanding of core consumer decision concepts to explain how individuals move from problem recognition to post purchase evaluation in cases and their final report.2.	Students will analyze how identity, self-concept, motivation, emotion, and attitude shape consumers’ judgments and choices across brands, campaigns, and technologies.3.	Students will evaluate how social influence and cultural norms guide consumers’ decisions within groups such as fandoms, peer networks, and online communities.4.	Students will assess how algorithms, AI, and digital interfaces structure consumers’ decision environments and affect attention, habits, and perceived control.5.	Students will examine and transform their assumptions about consumer choice and autonomy by critically synthesizing insights from real-world cases and evidence.
授業方法: All classes will be conducted entirely online through Zoom. Check the LUNA announcement for detailed instructions on how to join the online class.

第1回:
Course Introduction 
- Class operation and course evaluation
- Case assignments, team members and final report
- Foundations of Consumer Behavior and Choice

Please read the course material including assignments and final report available at LUNA (Content) before the class.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第2回:
Consumers as Individuals and Decision Makers:
- Motivation, perception, learning, attitudes; decision-making process; introduction to identity and social influence.
-Role of culture, social class, and reference groups in shaping consumption norms across markets.

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第3回: Case 1: Do Luxury Brands Engage in Excessive Profiteering?

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第4回:
- Perceived value, price–quality inference; cognitive dissonance and post-purchase justification; status motives and materialism
- Conspicuous consumption and inequality; ethical consumption, fairness norms, and generational attitudes toward luxury and profit.

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第5回: Case 2: TikTok’s Recommendation Algorithm – Influence on Consumer Behavior

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第6回:
- Heuristics and automatic processing; habit formation and intermittent rewards; attention, emotions, and micro-decision making.
- Social proof, virality, and peer influence; filter bubbles, youth culture, and cross-cultural content standards on platforms

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第7回: Case 3: Dove – The Real Beauty Campaign & Consumer Perspectives: Hit or Miss?

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第8回:
- Self-concept and self-discrepancy; body image and self-esteem; emotional appeals, schema congruity, and persuasion knowledge.
- Cultural beauty ideals and their psychological impact; authenticity vs purpose-washing in brand activism and feminism narratives.

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第9回: Case 4: Netflix and AI: The Invisible Director of Your Watchlist
Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
第10回:
- Choice architecture and nudging; perceived personalization and relevance; habit loops, binge-watching, and gratification seeking.
- Algorithmic shaping of cultural tastes; privacy, surveillance concerns, and consumer trust in global streaming platforms.

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第11回: Case 5: Humanizing Technology: Decoding Romi’s Appeal in Japan and the Future of Social Robotics

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第12回:
- Anthropomorphism; emotional attachment to non-human agents; technology acceptance and perceived warmth vs competence. 
- Japanese cultural norms toward robots; ethics, privacy boundaries, and social/psychological implications of relying on social robots.

Please read the course material available at LUNA (Content) and prepare for class discussion.
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
第13回: Please refer to the instructions for final report preparation
第14回: Course Review and Reflection - Integration of Consumers as Individuals and Decision Makers
About 100 minutes of self-study per session.
トピック.授業外学習2: Consumer Behavior course is highly recommended for marketing students to acquire a practical understanding of consumers as individuals and decision-makers. Business students who want to comprehend the underlying factors that influence people's behavior are also encouraged to take this course.

教科書/Required texts0: Michael R. Solomon, Consumer Behavior: Buying, Having and Being, Eighth Edition, Pearson Prentice Hall, 2009.
